Title: Set up Confluence/Jira epic
Description: Set up Confluence to collect information about the releaseJira epic under which the below issues are collected

Title: Define aim and version number
Description: Articulate the aim of the release, such as new resolution, new calibration, new model, new products etc. Important: avoid two major releases at the same time on the operational modelling chain + web service; avoid service and scientific updates at the same time and avoid clash with IFS-releases. Define a draft 'system' version number (e.g. GloFAS X.X).

Title: Identify changes to forcing data
Description: Identify changes required for forcing data (spatial/ temporal resolution, domain, type of input etc...), METEO, ERA, EUE/DInternally, we will be following the IFS cycles + ERA5 changes. We can use the esuites to test things in case we need to. As for Meteo, this is more on JRC's side. Check IFS release cycle.
Assignee: Christel Prudhomme Arthur Ramos Michel Wortmann

Title: Hydro observation data collection and quality check
Description: Hydro observation data collection and quality check. Coordinate with JRC and HDCC; includes mapping, checking attributes etc...Get updates from observational data from data centres (JRC, HDCC)Complete observation data collection and quality checkDepends on HDCC to deliver the data

Title: Forcing grids
Description: Review forcing grid generation and generate new forcing gridsAre we referring here to the pyg2p interpolation tables? They take time and should be done ASAP, but are only needed in case we change the resolution.

Title: Freeze Lisvap model version
Description: Freeze model version (LISFLOOD, LISVAP)

Title: Select and freeze calibration stations
Description: Select and freeze hydro model calibration stations

Title: Review and test calibration workflow
Description: Review, coordinate and test new calibration workflow with the JRCDepends on JRCs calibration method and workflow.

Title: Define the geospatial data version
Description: Define geospatial data used by lisfloodStart producing and organizing the files (moving things to vol)exclude time to generate the data which needs to have been achieved before
Assignee: Cinzia Mazzetti Ervin Zsoter Calum Baugh

Title: Plan MARS tree
Description: Discuss with MARS team (DADS-Team, Sebastien and Manuel)Variables to be ingested and identify those that would require a new GribCodePossible MARS tree layoutsnew WMO codes if needed

Title: Freeze version number for MARS
Description: Freeze 'system' version number (e.g. GloFAS 2.2) in order to be able to have it written in the MARS tree

Title: Model calibration
Description: Start calibration process about 18-12 before release, finish 6-9 months before release. Parameters maps need to be available.

Title: Define archiving strategy including data format
Description: Decide on archived variables on MARS and ecfs, coordinate with MARS team.Decide which variables to archiveArchive as many variables as possible on MARS so as not to depend too much on ECFSStart archiving from the beginning to prevent retrieving everything later to archive one or two variablesConsidering the volume, how are we archiving? By variable, by member, what format, etc. Do we need specific variables to compute other variables in the future?important for reforecasts as the volume is too big.
Assignee: Christel Prudhomme , Cinzia Mazzetti , Arthur Ramos Corentin Carton de Wiart

Title: Disk space for reference runs
Description: Review requirement and set up disk space for long-term run. Also plan disk space needed for reforecastsMost likely we will use /ec/vol/ to archive the forcings and the output. Keep it organized according to the version.This is to be able to conduct post-processing/ analysis and have a fast access

Title: Review XML parameter files for long term run
Description: Review XML filesSettings files for Lisvap and LisfloodAre they the same as the calibration? Should they be different or not? What variables do we want in the output? What state files do we want?Merge file from calibration and operational suite

Title: Review XML parameter files for reforecasts (medium range + seasonal)
Description: Review XML filesSettings files for Lisvap and LisfloodAre they the same as the longterm run? Should they be different or not? What variables do we want in the output? What state files do we want?Merge file from calibration and operational suite

Title: Long-term/reanalysis run
Description: Produce long-term (reanalysis) runForcing data - last time it was produced by JRC, We have the means to generate Lisvap output ourselvesforcings for Lisvap with data from MeteoRun Lisvap to generate Lisflood input - suite wb_clim.cfg To run the longtermrun: https://git.ecmwf.int/projects/CEFL/repos/longflow/browseDuration: two to three weeks as the years run in parallel. Give some extra days in case we need to fix things and/or rerun. Make sure all variables to be archived are output and not deletedIncludes thresolds calculations/ climatology
Assignee: Corentin Carton de Wiart Arthur Ramos

Title: Medium-range, subseasonal and seasonal reforecasts forcings
Description: Start downloading forcings for reforecasts to prepare for the reforecastssub-seasonal only needs 3 dates and 6 weeks of reforecastsseasonal 6 months reforecasts + 1 monthsmedium-range reforecasts for 3 months

Title: Impacts on products dependant on long-term run
Description: Identify impacts on any products dependant on long-term run:Flood thresholdsERIC climatology needs re-computing if LISFLOOD is re-calibrated → CalumRapid risk needs reviewing if model timestep and/or grid is changed → Calum
Assignee: Juan Pereira Colonese , Corentin Carton de Wiart

Title: Evaluate long-term/reanalysis run
Description: Evaluate long-term run - hydrological model performanceAcceptance testIn case of degraded results, consult the JRC to decide on release options

Title: Review XML parameter files for forecasts (medium range + seasonal)
Description: Review XML filesSettings files for Lisvap and LisfloodAre they the same as the longterm run? Should they be different or not? What variables do we want in the output? What state files do we want?Merge file from calibration and operational suite

Title: New layers tested in website
Description: Inform web developer about new layers/ changes and test them

Title: PP calibration stations
Description: Select and freeze PP calibration stations

Title: Freeze evaluation strategy
Description: Freeze evaluation strategyWhat is evaluated and how, metrics usedQuick- evaluation method (relying on short archived forecasts)

Title: Test prototype layers on dev
Description: First prototype layers on dev website (GloFAS and EFAS)

Title: Install assets/static files
Description: At this point, we should have all (or most) of the assets already copied to the test machine so it can be used by the product generation. This is to avoid copying/moving TB of data just before the release. We can always change/update the files, but the heavy stuff should be there on ws4/ws3 with the right versions. Assets from:Lisflood RapidMapping (Global_RRA, rapid_mapping)pp (ppData)Pyg2pseasonal (seData)TODOReview naming conventions for the assets (same set of files with different names for GloFAs and EFAS)Review directory structure for different versionsMaybe merge EFAS/GloFAS assets?Document where the data comes from
Assignee: Juan Pereira Colonese , Arthur Ramos , Calum Baugh , Cinzia Mazzetti

Title: Review XML files
Description: Review XML files for the MRSettings files for Lisvap and LisfloodAre they the same as the WB? Should they be different or not? What variables do we want in the output? What state files do we want?Avoid errors we had in the past with wrong XML files.

Title: E-suite
Description: Create and run an e-suite in real time.

Title: Freeze verification point list
Description: Freeze verification point list

Title: Medium-range + sub-seasonal reforecasts
Description: Generate medium-range reforecast (medium-range + sub-seasonal; dependant on long run and splitting), and output as GRIB files (without dummy model date) and save on HPCSubtasks:Save on disk all discharge relevant reforecast dataDuration: around 3 monthsDefine reference date for the reforecasts to startShould start as soon as longterm run is archived on ECFSfor EFAS: suite efhc.cfg, ECFS: /emos/efas/efhc/for GloFAS: prod_glofas_rf.cfg, ECFS: /emos/glofas/glofas_reforecast_4.0/Includes archiving to MARSIncludes threshold calculationbut keep on disk for evaluation

Title: Seasonal reforecasts
Description: Generate seasonal reforecast (dependant on long run and splitting), and output as GRIB files (without dummy model date) and save on HPCSubtasks:Save on disk all discharge relevant reforecast dataDuration: around 2 monthsShould start as soon as the longterm run is archived on ECFSfor EFAS: suite efas_seas_sc.cfg or efms.cfg (monthly + seasonal mixed together), on ECFS: /emos/efas/efas_seas_5.0 (to be confirmed)for GloFAS: suite prod_glofas_seas_reforecast.cfg, on ECFS: /emos/glofas/glofas_seasonal_reforecast_4.0Includes archiving to MARSalso keeps on diskincludes threshold calculation

Title: Flashflood climatology
Description: Generate ERIC/other flashflood model climatology maps (dependant on long run)

Title: Retrieve Flashflood reforecasts forcings
Description: Usually the bottleneck for Flashflood reforecasts is the retrieval time of COSMO-LEPS forcing data. We can start as soon as possible

Title: Test and show new products on stage
Description: New products available in stage/dev system (depending on the testing)

Title: Generate test forecasts
Description: Generate test historical 'real-time' forecasts (e.g. first starts past 1 year) without products

Title: Water Balance Suite
Description: Start Water Balance Suite to fill gap between end of long term run and start of test suiteDuration: less than a month. We can use the longterm run to catch up faster (less than a week), but there are still things to fix in the suite. The duration also depends on the reference date we will use as the end of the longtermrun and start of WB suite

Title: PP calibration
Description: prepare all inputs from long term run and start PP calibration

Title: Internal user testing
Description: Testing by users of the new products to start. TO DEFINE the group of people which we want to ask for their opinion in fine-tuning the products!

Title: Quick evaluation of benchmark forecasts
Description: Conduct quick evaluation of forecasts 1 year of benchmark forecast. In case of degraded results, consult the JRC to decide on release options.

Title: User testing
Description: Tell super users of new release details (major release only), e.g. tell Aristotle to start looking at GloFAS test for new products if relevant/available.

Title: Release article
Description: Finalise the web article for the EFAS/GloFAS website about the release

Title: Define static data for product generation version
Description: Define and acquire data needed for product generation

Title: Finalise station and river network
Description: Geospatial data after PP updates:Freeze station list, will depend on HDCCRelease efas_river_network_5km.shp shapefile into stage

Title: Flashflood reforecasts
Description: Generate 1 year of ERIC reforecasts and evaluate different exceedance probability thresholds for flash flood notifications

Title: Fix reporting points
Description: Freeze fixed reporting point list

Title: Long-term run archiving
Description: Once we are happy with the results/thresholds/analysis, we can archive the longterm run:Archiving:Define the reference date to be used in MARS and ECFS (e.g. DATE=20230101,)MARS - The output of the longterm run comes in one big netCDF by variable file split by years. The files can be archived using the full netCDF file. DATE=$reference_dateECFS - There's a ECFS task on the suite that splits the files in WB${date} so it can be easily archived in ECFSpath on ecfs: /emos/efas/clim $reference_dateMove/copy the files to /ec/vol/ in the right locationeverything needs to be kept on disk for evaluation

Title: Final adjustments to new product layers
Description: The finishing touches for new product layers, including legend image and text finalised

Title: Freeze web sites
Description: Web sites: changes frozen, no changes allowed, unless bug/something critical

Title: Update map server
Description: GloFAS-IS map serverWeb Ops to update GloFAS-IS testJRC to update GloFAS-IS test

Title: Synchronize static and output files
Description: Synchronize static and output files (ws4 to ws3)

Title: Monitor E-suite
Description: Team to monitor performance of e-suite system and inform of bugs

Title: Production suite
Description: Production suiteStart preparation (day before)Copy production static fields to legacy/ production suites file systemsCopy production workdir into legacy suite (i.e. previous operational suite)Copy current test suite workdir into production folderSend operational change note

Title: Test MARS archiving
Description: By now we should have defined all the variables we want to archive, we should have them on ecCodes/MARS, and DADS team should have it revised accordingly. First start archiving on MARS Scratch, see if the data makes sense, test different variables.Start archiving on marser with a different expver (9901, 9999) so it can be tested on CDS.

Title: Update CEMS-Flood Wiki
Description: CEMS-Flood WikiMake new pages publicMake sure existing pages are updated to refer to the new release (e.g. EFAS versioning system and GloFAS versioning system)

Title: Web site release branch
Description: Web sites: create release branch (to check with JRC for GloFAS)Make sure new entry added to efas_version_version table, to be verified at https://www.efas.eu/api/version/version/Take backup of static shp & related files and copy new ones there (in efas@glofas-dissemination-prod from /data/efas-stage/shp to /data/efas/shp etc)Turn on banner to warn users of upcoming release (with updated date/time)

Title: Update Change Management
Description: Book System Session (operations + web) so Change Management are aware; release date needs to be final at this point

Title: Flashflood documentation
Description: Write documentation about evaluation of new ERIC climatology, send report to DISS & JRC. Plan a telco to discuss the results

Title: CDS release
Description: New datasets: ask CDS to move complete dataset from CDS-test into CDS production environmentExisting datasets: ask CDS to move files from CDS-test into CDS production environment

Title: Web site release
Description: GloFAS-IS map serverWeb Ops to be know when production is doneTell JRCEFAS-IS: full release process

Title: Activate EFAS stations version on Floods-DB
Description: Activate EFAS stations version on Floods-DB (geoDB)

Title: Deploy legacy suites
Description: Legacy suites to be deployedUpdate Archiving ECFS/MARSUpdate Static Location (file path, storage unit, etc..) (if changed)

Title: PP test suite
Description: Start test suiteSubtasks:Generate back forecasts for PP spin-upTest SOS ingestion using PP dataSpinup PP Suite. Enable PP in EFAS Test Suite (Or Use EFAS Test suite to create its own spinup)

Title: Outreach/Announcement
Description: Complete 5 weeks before release:Final draft Wiki documentation ready (restricted access)Send Wiki pages for review to critical identified people (JRC, Dis, etc...) (provide access)Complete 2 weeks before releaseFinal edits to Wiki documentation following external reviewSend for JRC review outreach material (news item, newsletter, )

Title: Conduct reforecasts evaluation
Description: generate benchmark forecasts and compare with reforecasts to assess forecast skill + produce evaluation forecast layout

Title: Inform Aristotle
Description: Aristotle: inform them about any changes to the rapid impact assessment products

Title: Prepare CDS upload
Description: Review existing CEMS-Flood datatset and add new requirements : https://efascom.smhi.se/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=54460663inform CDS team of upcoming datasets (for new datasets, different variables, different projection etc...)Submit dataset documentation for CDS review and approval includes: An Abstract for the overview page, this declares what the dataset is, who generated it and why it is a valuable resource to users.An Overview Image that represents the data, such as a historical timeseries or an area plot.A list of variables to be published and description.Submit a manifest file for review (This is in the form of the mars.list or list-study.txt)

Title: Product generation suite tasks
Description: Plug product generation in test suite and start debugging
Assignee: Corentin Carton de Wiart , Juan Pereira Colonese

Title: Archiving of ECFS data to MARS
Description: Start pulling data off ECFS for archiving.This could be reforecasts or reference runs.Not sure here what is to be pulled or archived.

Title: Publish news article
Description: GloFAS web news article & Tweet about the release (CopernicusEMS)

Title: Score database and archive all results
Description: Create a score database and archive all results (forecast verification + model performance). Updated score database and archive all results 5-4 months before again. Conduct final evaluation (for scores needing reforecasts completed).The final evaluation code to be shared as tables as well as evaluation layer to provide to users who want it. In case of degraded results, consult the JRC to decide on release options.
